ground_truth.title: Food Prices for Nutrition DataHub: global statistics on the Cost and Affordability of Healthy Diets
ground_truth.main_content: The Food Prices for Nutrition DataHub provides access to global statistics on the cost and affordability of healthy diets and related indicators. These data use food item availability and prices from the International Comparison Program (ICP), combined with food composition data and nutritional requirements from the Healthy Diet Basket based on national dietary guidelines. The 42 indicators cover diet cost (cost of a healthy diet, nutrient adequate diet, and energy sufficient diet per person per day) and diet affordability (prevalence of unaffordability and number of people unable to afford each diet). In 2024, the average cost of a healthy diet reached $4.46 per person per day globally, and around 2.6 billion people could not afford a healthy diet. Sub-Saharan Africa and low-income countries faced two consecutive years of worsening affordability since 2022. The most expensive food group is animal-sourced foods at $1.00 on average, with vegetables second at $0.76 globally. Food Prices for Nutrition was established to provide governments and development agencies with accurate metrics to inform agricultural, food systems, social protection, and health interventions. The cost and affordability indicators underlie estimates in the UN's State of Food Security and Nutrition in the World reports and support monitoring of SDG 2 to end hunger.
with: The Food Prices for Nutrition DataHub provides access to global statistics on the cost and affordability of healthy diets and related indicators.
The 42 indicators and their metadata cover diet cost and diet affordability metrics across 170+ countries.
In 2024, the average cost of a healthy diet reached $4.46 per person per day globally.
Around 2.6 billion people could not afford a healthy diet in 2024, with Sub-Saharan Africa facing worsening affordability trends.
The most expensive food group is animal-sourced foods at $1.00, followed by vegetables at $0.76 globally.
